title: No injuries after drone hits U.S. consulate in Dubai, Rubio says
author: CBS News
fullText: We begin with breaking news out of the Middle East. Sources telling CBS News the US consulate in Dubai was hit by a drone. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on Capitol Hill right now briefing Congress on the ongoing operations in Iran. He updated reporters on the incident in Dubai just moments ago. Take a listen. The last update I had with seconds before getting before these cameras was that a drone unfortunately struck a parking lot adjacent to the to the chancellory building and then set off a fire in that place. All personnel are accounted for. As you're aware, we began drawing down personnel from our diplomatic facilities uh in advance of this. In the cases, for example, of Beirut, we basically drew down to bare bones as well as in as as in uh Baghdad and in Herbiel and in a couple other posts as well. So, um we've been very fortunate obviously, but our embassies and our diplomatic facilities are under direct attack from a terroristic regime. >> Let's get right to CBS News correspondent Chanel Call. Chanel, what more can you tell us about this? >> Okay, so let's go through what we have confirmed for you at this moment, Liz. Uh the Dubai media office on X sending up this message saying Dubai authorities have confirmed that a fire resulting from a drone related incident near the US consulate has been successfully contained. Emergency teams they say responded immediately. No injuries have been reported. And you heard from Secretary of State Marco Rubio there confirming to us Liz uh saying all personnel are accounted for at this point. uh and that unfortunately he says this struck a parking lot adjacent to the chancellor really building and then set a fire in that place. Uh we're also getting some video like what you're seeing on your screen right now that's really showing the fire and smoke that erupted on the consulate grounds. We also have some video showing onlookers, you know, watching this huge plume of smoke. So certainly some scary moments there. >> Absolutely. And I want to talk a little bit more about where this is in the heart of the city of Dubai. very popular with tourists and it's not far from the Burj Khalifa which is one of the iconic buildings in Dubai. Can you talk a little bit more about that? >> I mean the Burj Khalifa if you haven't been to Dubai it's really the anchor of downtown Dubai and here you go there's a map and that will show you just how close it is. It's about 15 minutes of a drive north of the bridge Khalifa. That's where the US consulate building sits. Um, and it's also near the Palm, which is, you know, that famous man-made island, Liz, home to a lot of luxury hotels. Now, just imagine if you're an American on the ground there, you're seeing this happen after the the Dubai airport was also struck. We also know that luxury Fairmont Hotel in the Bay was struck. So, I actually was just speaking to an American who's there. I interviewed her earlier today and on Sunday as well. And on Sunday, Liz, she seemed pretty calm, pretty chill. She was open to being quite patient. And then now look what she's seen. Three huge sites have been struck by Iranian missiles. She is terrified. Um I just text her now and she said this. We as Americans are the target. It's not a great feeling to be in a foreign destination and know you cannot take safety at your own consulate because your existence there is a safety concern. And you remember that the State Department was saying last night, uh, if you are in 14 Middle Eastern countries, please get out. Use commercial services to get back home. And I was asking her about that. She's saying, look, there aren't any options to get out. She was trying to get on a plane. Those tickets are costing something like $12,000. Uh, the only other options are try to try to drive somewhere else, uh, like Riad or Oman to try to get a plane out of there. But Oman was also hit yesterday. So certainly a very fluid situation, but the conflict getting hit in Dubai, that is going to be frightening for any Americans who are stuck there right now. >> Absolutely. And there is a sizable population of Americans that do live in Dubai because of the businesses that are located there. Chanel Colin are going to keep us updated on the situation. Thank you so much.
